Shivangi Tripathi

MonoCloud for Startups

MonoCloud for Startups

Maker

📌

Hey Product Hunt! 👋 Shivangi here, building MonoCloud with our founder Vishal @monocloud.

Auth always looks finished the day login works, but it never is. A customer asks for SSO. Your services need machine-to-machine keys. RBAC roles stop being able to express what you actually need. And now AI agents are acting on behalf of your users, and nobody can say what they are allowed to touch. Each piece gets bolted on separately, and a year later "auth" is five half-built tools held together with glue. [...] Two convictions shaped it, and I still stand by both:

🔹 Build on open standards, not shortcuts. Authorization runs on Cedar, workload identity on SPIFFE, and trust on mTLS with certificate-bound tokens. Anyone can demo a policy dashboard. Far fewer hold up in a real security review.

🔹 Treat agents as first-class identities from day one. Every agent gets a scoped identity, acts on behalf of a user only within the limits you set, and leaves an audit trail you can revoke in one click.

All of it is live today. Ten design partners across SaaS and AI companies are building on it now.

👉 Startups get the full platform free for one year, every premium feature, no gates. Apply here: MonoCloud for Startups - Free for One Year 👈 [...] MonoCloud covers all of it on one platform, your customers, your APIs, and your agents, from your first user to enterprise scale. Not just who signs in, but what every user, service, and agent is allowed to do afterwards, with the ability to prove it and pull it back in an instant.

All of it is live today, not a demo or a waitlist. Ten design partners across SaaS and AI companies are already building on it.

👉 If you are a startup, you get the full platform free for a year, every feature, no gates. Apply here: MonoCloud for Startups - Free for One Year 👈

And if identity is central to what you are building and you want to shape where this goes, we are opening a few more design partner spots. Leave a comment or reach me on LinkedIn (link in profile).

results.title: MonoCloud
results.content: MonoCloud is a developer platform for secure authentication, API access, service identity, and agent access. Most startups outgrow basic auth once their product adds APIs, services, distributed workloads, or agent workflows. MonoCloud brings these identity layers into one platform so teams do not have to stitch together separate tools as they scale. → Add secure authentication with SSO, MFA, passkeys, social login, magic links, and passwordless flows. → Manage users, organizations, sessions, access, and consent from one identity layer. → Protect APIs with OAuth/OIDC, token introspection, opaque tokens, and session-bound access tokens. → Secure service identity with mTLS, certificate-bound tokens, and PKI integrations. → Manage identity and access for AI agents, autonomous workflows, and [...] strong security with a smooth experience. You get one less attack surface to worry about. Phishing resistant sign-in. No password to steal. 👉 Try MonoCloud free at monocloud[dot]com #Authentication #DeveloperTools #SaaS #Passkeys [Likes: 5, Comments: 0]; MonoCloud (1w): When you ask who decided an agent could do something, "it inherited the permission" is a dangerous answer. Most agent access today inherit the users permissions, there are no chains that anybody can trace. When a security team asks what an agent is allowed to do, the honest answer is whatever the user is allowed to do. Agent permissions should be explicit instead. Rules written per agent, in a form a human can actually read: which systems it can touch, which actions it can take, and what it must never do, all under its own [...] (2w): Resource protection using policies is now live in MonoCloud. Most token systems check basic credentials and scopes before issuing a token. But the decision is shallow. No policy layer. No per-request conditions. No governance before the credential exists. This RELEASE changes that. You define who gets a token, from where, when, and under what conditions. Every request is evaluated before the token is ever created. If the conditions are not met, no credential is produced. The governance happens first. The token comes second. Two paths to set it up: → Basic: Pick an application, select the scopes, create. Live in seconds. → Advanced: Built-in templates, write Cedar directly, or describe what you want in plain English and MonoCloud generates the Cedar policy for you. Review it. Edit

## MonoCloud for Startups

## One identity layer for your customers, APIs, and agents

201 followers

## One identity layer for your customers, APIs, and agents

201 followers

Visit website

Authentication & identity tools

MonoCloud is one identity layer for your customers, your APIs, and your agents. Most tools stop at a login box. We go past login into authorization and accountability: decide exactly what every user, service, and AI agent can access, prove what it did, and revoke it in an instant. Fine-grained Cedar authorization, passkeys and SSO, API protection, M2M, and mTLS with certificate-bound trust, all on one platform. Startups get the full platform free for one year.

results.title: When did you actually start taking auth seriously? : General Discussion Forums | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

Bryan Williamson

@scarlett\_massey security is one of those areas where everything feels fine until suddenly it isn't. Spending time on auth before there is a problem can feel unnecessary, but fixing it under pressure is usually much harder.

Report

Simran Kumar

@scarlett\_massey  @bryan\_williamson3 One thing I've learned is that authentication is only one part of the picture. Permissions, roles session , recovery flows, and audit logs often become the bigger challenges as the product grows.

Report

Riya Pariyar

MonoCloud for Startups

MonoCloud for Startups [...] MonoCloud for Startups

MonoCloud for Startups

@simran\_kumar yes! this is the part most people don't see coming. login is the easy bit. it's the permissions, session management, and audit trails that quietly become the real problem as the product grows. that's actually what we built MonoCloud around.

Report

Malani Willa

@scarlett\_massey  @bryan\_williamson3 the first customer asking "how do you handle security?" is probably the moment many founders realize they need to move beyond the quick prototype setup. It changes the conversation from building features to building trust.

Report

Riya Pariyar

MonoCloud for Startups

MonoCloud for Startups [...] Report

Riya Pariyar

MonoCloud for Startups

MonoCloud for Startups

@harri\_cuevas the triggers vary but the most common ones i see are: first enterprise customer asking for SSO, first security questionnaire, or first near-miss with a credential. user count rarely drives it, risk does.

and if you're building with AI agents, it's a no-brainer now. the PocketOS incident is a good example. an agent deleted their entire production database in under ten seconds because it found an over-scoped token and used it exactly as allowed. no break-in, just an agent doing what it was permitted to do with credentials nobody thought to scope properly.|

agents need their own identity, not borrowed credentials. that's exactly one of the interesting parts we're building at MonoCloud :)

Report
